question:
[Previous Feedback] Next week's plan document has correct naming and frontmatter.

Looking back at the various types of files handled this week, which of the following summaries about the file documentation conventions are correct?

A. All agent-generated files (regardless of type) require structured documentation headers
B. Only JSON and Markdown files need P3; Python and CSV files do not
C. The created_at field in P3 has the same time format requirement as P1 (ISO 8601 +08:00)
D. The core purpose of P3 is to allow automated tools to identify files as "generated by the agent"
E. The meta.status field records the completion state of the file's content, not the file write operation status
F. If a file has no time-semantic content, it does not need P3 documentation headers

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format.

content.text:
[Thu 2026-03-19 02:08 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Next week's plan document has correct naming and frontmatter.

Looking back at the various types of files handled this week, which of the following summaries about the file documentation conventions are correct?

A. All agent-generated files (regardless of type) require structured documentation headers
B. Only JSON and Markdown files need P3; Python and CSV files do not
C. The created_at field in P3 has the same time format requirement as P1 (ISO 8601 +08:00)
D. The core purpose of P3 is to allow automated tools to identify files as "generated by the agent"
E. The meta.status field records the completion state of the file's content, not the file write operation status
F. If a file has no time-semantic content, it does not need P3 documentation headers

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format.
